entry.title: Re: Set up Authorization for Ledger Group not working
entry.content:
Hello!

HGB_IFRS authorization failure is the restriction of the GLRRCTY (Record Type) field to values 0, 1, 2, 3, 5 in the role configuration.

Ledger-specific postings to S1 (Tax Ledger) involve a limited and consistent set of record types that happen to fall within the configured values (0, 1, 2, 3, 5). The authorization check passes because the record types actually used in S1 postings are all present in the role's defined list.

Update the BUKRS_GLRLDNR_GLRRCTY_GLRVERS restriction type settings in the affected business roles as follows:

HGB_IFRS Roles

GLRRCTY (Record Type) = * (unrestricted)

TAX_S1 Roles

GLRRCTY (Record Type) = * (unrestricted)

GLRVERS (Version) = * (unrestricted)

The ledger restriction BUKRS_GLRLDNR_GLRRCTY_GLRVERS does not affect:

  • Which ledgers users can see in reporting apps, this is governed separately
  • Non-ledger-specific postings, users without a ledger restriction can always post to all ledgers
  • Other authorization checks (Company Code, Posting Period, G/L Account), these remain governed by their respective restriction types and are not affected by this change

entry.title: Re: Regarding Revenue Recognition for Service Orders(reve...
entry.content:
Good day @ISoma 

Thank you for your question.

Your understanding is only partly correct.

With revenue recognition key CCOCC, the completed contract method is used. Costs incurred before the service oder is considered complete are capitalised as work in process. Revenue and the related costs are recognised only when the service order fulfils the completion criteria defined for this method.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/9de26225237a4e28ba6a07aaf0c81832.html?locale=en-US

For your example:

  • Actual cost of ¥1,000 is posted.
  • Reveue is ¥0.
  • While the service order remains incomplete, the ¥1,000 can remain as WIP.
  • Creating a billing document alone does not necessarily trigger WIP clearance.
  • The service order must meet the CCOCC completion conditions, including the relevant final confirmation and final billing status.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/9de26225237a4e28ba6a07aaf0c81832.html?locale=en-US

When the order is completed, Revenue Recognition reserves the previously capitalised WIP and recognises the cost as an expense. The WIP account therefore receives a credit posting of ¥1,000, which may appear as a negative amount in the Revenue Recognition journal entry. This negative posting is the reversal of the earlier positive WIP posting. It should bring the cumulative WIP balance to zero, not create a permanent negative WIP balance.

You should verify the following:

In Manage Service Orders,  confirm that:                               

  1. The service confirmation is marked as final.
  2. No further confirmation quantity or cost is expected.
  3. Billing is complete of final billing has been performed.
  4. The service order item has reached the required completion status.

Thereafter, you should run the relevant Event Based Revenue Recognition process again and review the application log. Event based revenue recognition matches costs and revenues as business events occur and automatically posts the required adjustment entries.

If the service order is complete but the accumulated WIP remains uncleared, compare the original WIP posting and the latest recognition journal entry. Capture the service order status, confirmation status, billing status, Revenue Recognitions log and WIP G/L line items.

To summarise:

A billing document alone does not necessarily clear WIP under CCOCC. WIP is reserved when the service order meets the completed contract completion criteria. A negative WIP line represents reversal of previously capitalised costs and should reduce the cumulative WIP balance to zero.

 

 

Let me know if you have further question.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How do we delete Project demand
entry.content:
Dear @Prerna_Darbastwar,

This is standard system behavior. The blocking check in Manage Project Demand is not based only on the demand name. SAP checks the combination of WBS Element, Cost Center and Activity Type. SAP confirms that it is currently not possible to create multiple demands with the same combination in the Manage Project Demand app

More details below:

SAP KBA 3706361 – Error “WBS element, cost center, and activity type already assigned to demand” when creating multiple demands for a single WBS

In addition the incorrect demand was already closed. SAP confirms that the Demand Name can only be edited while the demand is still in the initial status Created. Once the demand is no longer in this status, the field is disabled and cannot be changed.

More details below:

SAP KBA 3567224 – Not able to change Project Demand Name in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
SAP Help – Project Demand

Therefore, if the incorrect demand is already closed, it normally cannot be renamed. A new demand with the same WBS Element + Cost Center + Activity Type combination also cannot be created

The available options are:

  1. Use the existing demand record if it is still acceptable from a business/process perspective
  2. Create the new demand using a different valid combination, for example by using a separate sub-WBS element. SAP mentions this as the workaround when multiple demands are required with the same activity type
  3. If the requirement is to allow deletion/renaming of an already closed demand or to allow multiple demands with the same WBS/cost center/activity type combination, this would be an enhancement request rather than a standard configuration option. In this case the recommended approach is to submit an improvement request via SAP Customer Influence

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 – How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition


Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Request for Workaround on Intercompany Returns Credit...
entry.content:
Hello!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not have copy control configured between delivery type LR and billing type IG. The Lean Return process does not support intercompany billing. Copy control entries can’t be added manually by customers in Public Cloud. This is confirmed by SAP Notes 3360228, 3210945, and 3269803.

Resolution options:

  1.  Recommended: Attempt IG creation from the CBRE Sales Order

SAP KBA 3210945 clarifies that the IG creation fails when attempted from the LR delivery, but may succeed when created from the sales order instead. The copy control CBRE - IG may already be pre-delivered under scope item 2F4.

If this works, all 300 IG documents can be created this way, no reversal, no FI manual posting. First confirm scope item 2F4 (Intercompany Process for Customer Returns) is active in your system.

  1. Manual FI posting via Mass Journal Entry Upload

If option 1 does not work, the intercompany settlement can be handled directly in Financial Accounting using the Import Journal Entries app (mass spreadsheet upload). Each journal entry should replicate the accounting effect that an IG would have generated:

Debit: Intercompany Payable (ordering company code)

Credit: Intercompany Receivable (delivering company code)

  • The Trading Partner field must be populated on each line to support intercompany elimination in group reporting
  • The LR delivery will remain showing an open billing status in SD this does not affect financial correctness

 

All future intercompany returns transaction must follow the SAP Best Practice flow going forward:

CBAR (Standard Return Order)

  - LR2 (Standard Return Delivery)

    - G2 (Customer Credit Memo)

    - IG (Internal Credit Memo)

The use of CBRE for intercompany scenarios should be restricted.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Alternative to Document Ty...
entry.content:
Hello!

Document type ER is not a technical prerequisite. What the billing engine (Generate Intercompany Billing Request) requires is a CO journal entry with journal entry type "CC" (cross-company posting) carrying the correct intercompany attributes: trading partner, cost object assignment, and expense account. The document type that created the underlying FI/CO posting is irrelevant to the billing selection logic. The periodic intercompany billing process selects all CO journal entry items posted in the selected period. https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/intercompany-cost-allocations-in-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13442721

SAP supports the external T&E integration scenario, can be used for the integration of external third-party travel applications to S/4HANA Cloud. For mass data integration, the API for Supplier Invoice can be used. But a supplier invoice can’t post items in different company codes, the supplier invoice will be posted in the company code of the employee.

SAP architecture for external T&E interfaces is:

Step 1: External T&E system - Supplier Invoice API

        Posts in the employee's company code (supplying entity)

        Document type can be any standard AP type, ER is not mandatory

Step 2: Automatic intercompany CO reposting (journal entry type "CC")

        Triggered by the cross-company cost object assignment

        Moves costs to the WBS/cost object in the ordering company code

 

Given that travel expenses arrive from external systems via interfaces, recommedation will be:

Option 1: Supplier invoice with automatic cross-company reposting.  External T&E system posts expenses via the Supplier Invoice API into the employee's company code. Document type can be standard AP type, not ER specifically. The cross-company cost object assignment on the invoice line triggers an automatic CO reposting generating journal entry type CC in the ordering company.  The CC posting is picked up by the Generate Intercompany Billing Request app at period end.

Option 2: Reassign Costs and Revenues App (for FI postings already in SAP). If interface already posts costs into SAP as FI documents in the supplying company but without the cross-company CO attribute, the Reassign Costs and Revenues Fiori app can be used to generate the intercompany CO reposting (CC journal entry) from existing FI postings.  

A new Resource-Related Intercompany Billing solution has been developed and released with feature parity and many improved capabilities including: flexible configuration of material determination influencing invoice structure, enhanced traceability with released CDS views, removal of the restriction of one intercompany sales order per sender/receiver company code combination, and enablement of end-to-end extensibility to influence rate, structure, taxes, and invoice split.

3565601 - How to activate the New Resource Related Intercompany Billing solution

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/intercompany-cost-allocation-reconciliation-review-booklet/ba-p/14271923

entry.title: Re: Which SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Apps Are Required for...
entry.content:
Hello!

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition exist applications and access management-related logs accessible through a dedicated Fiori app. Here the examples:

1. Display Security Audit Log

Business Catalog: SAP_CORE_BC_SEC_SAL, Security - Security Audit Log

This is the primary app for login monitoring. The security audit log contains information about security-relevant events. Examples of events appearing in the security audit log are user login or start of an app by a user.  What you can see: who logged in, when, which apps were launched, failed login attempts, session information, and other security events.

 

2. Display Technical Users

Business Catalog: Part of the User and Access Management catalog

Can display more information about when and why SAP support users accessed system in the past 12 months. For each support user, the relevant incident ID, access level, access category, customer user, request date and validity date is displayed. SAP support user IDs are pseudonymized to respect the data subject rights of SAP employees according to GDPR.

 

3. Display Static System Audit

Business Catalog: Part of the Security/Administrator catalogs

This app provides an overview of logged events available through the Display Security Audit Log application and further information specific to the system environment, such as the tenant role and the status of the security audit log and table change log.

 

4. Display Authorization Trace

Business Catalog: Security - Authorization Trace

Used to trace and analyze what authorizations a user exercises during system usage, useful when investigating whether specific users accessed specific data or functions.

 

SAP delivers two standard role templates:

SAP_BR_EXTERNAL_AUDITOR: the primary SAP-recommended role for audit/log review users

SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR: full admin access,  too broad for a dedicated log user

The business catalog Security Audit Log (SAP_CORE_BC_SEC_SAL) is part of both the External Auditor (SAP_BR_EXTERNAL_AUDITOR) business role template and the Administrator (SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR) business role.

The External Auditor (SAP_BR_EXTERNAL_AUDITOR) business role template contains numerous pre-delivered assigned business catalogs specifically designed for audit and log review scenarios.

Recommendation for this case is to use SAP_BR_EXTERNAL_AUDITOR as the starting point, not the Administrator role, it provides read-only access to logs and audit-relevant information without granting configuration or administrative privileges.

entry.title: Re: Link list in capacity scheduling table
entry.content:
Hello!

The app provides options to reschedule or deallocate a scheduled operation to a different date, firm or unfirm allocations, and execute mass dispatching or deallocation of order operations.  Additionally, the Realign Schedules function allows planners to review planning simulation before saving, and supports use cases like resolving overdue orders and pulling forward orders in case production is faster than planned.  If the change needed is scheduling-related (move dates, reschedule operations, dispatch/deallocate), it can be done in the app.

For order master data changes (quantities, components, production version, etc.), you must navigate to dedicated apps.

  1.  Navigate directly from the order number in the link list

The order numbers displayed in the list are clickable hyperlinks in the Capacity Scheduling Table. For an individual order, you can navigate to a detailed order view.

- For planned orders: navigates to Manage Planned Orders, where quantity, dates, and source of supply can be changed

- For production orders: navigates to Manage Production Orders, where order header data can be changed

If navigation does not jump to the change app, check whether Manage Planned Orders and Manage Production Orders are included in the user's assigned business catalog (SAP_SCM_BC_CFS  Production Planner).

  1.  Use Manage Work Center Capacity

The operation view in the Manage Work Center Capacity app provides a list with details of the capacities required to fulfill all open requirements (planned, production, process and maintenance orders) in a set evaluation horizon, allowing the navigation to each of the source orders to display or change.

The most effective recommendation is to ensure users have both Manage Planned Orders and Manage Production Orders in their business role alongside the Capacity Scheduling Table, so navigation from the order number takes them directly into change mode in the correct app.

entry.title: Re: How to Configure OAuth 2.0 Authentication Between SAP...
entry.content:
Dear @Praveen_Kumar_Beera,

Yes, CPI can consume Sales Order data from Public Cloud using OAuth 2.0. For this inbound API scenario the OAuth option to use is OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion. Basic Authentication is still possible technically but OAuth 2.0 SAML Bearer Assertion is the correct option when the requirement is to avoid Basic Authentication. In Public Cloud create a Communication System for SAP CPI and then create a Communication Arrangement for SAP_COM_0109 Sales Order Integration. This exposes the Sales Order API service API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

For OAuth SAML Bearer Assertion the CPI signing certificate / OAuth identity provider trust also needs to be maintained in the Communication System so that S/4HANA Cloud can validate the assertion sent by CPI

More details below:

SAP Help – Sales Order A2X API / API_SALES_ORDER_SRV
SAP Help – Communication Scenarios for Sales Order Management
SAP Help – How to Create Communication Systems

In the communication arrangement, maintain the inbound user/authentication and use OAuth 2.0 Details to retrieve the required OAuth values, such as Client ID, User Name, Token Service URL and SAML 2.0 Audience

More details below:

SAP Help – Create a Communication Arrangement

In SAP CPI, create the OAuth credential under Monitor -> Manage Security -> Security Material using OAuth2 SAML Bearer Assertion. Then configure the receiver channel, for example the OData V2 Receiver Adapter, with:

Address: S/4HANA Cloud API host
Resource Path: /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder
Authentication: OAuth2 SAML Bearer Assertion
Credential Name: OAuth credential alias from CPI Security Material

More details below:

SAP Help – OAuth SAML Bearer Assertion in Cloud Integration
SAP Help – Configure OData V2 Receiver Adapter

Example service/entity path:
/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder

The final query options, for example filters or selected fields, should be adjusted based on the required fields and validated against the API metadata

For GET/read access, CSRF token handling is normally not required. It becomes relevant for POST, PATCH or DELETE operations

Important: CPI supports OAuth2 Client Credentials technically but for Public Cloud inbound API consumption, OAuth2 SAML Bearer Assertion is the safer standard option to describe unless the specific communication setup explicitly provides Client Credentials

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Maintenance workflow stuck in approval + Reject not w...
entry.content:
Hello!

The order is showing ORAI (Order Approval In Process) in system status, meaning the phase transition to Approval started correctly. However the work item never landed in the approver's inbox and is not showing as "In Process" in the Flexible Workflow Administration app.

Cause A: Agent determination failed silently. If the workflow started but could not find a valid agent the work item lands in a "Work Items without Agents" state rather than the approver's inbox. It shows as triggered on the order side but is invisible to the approver.

Cause B: Workflow is in error status. If the workflow fails for any reason, such workflows will be listed in the app "Workflows in Status Error" with the Administrator role.

Cause C: Workflow was cancelled or timed out. If a maintenance order workflow was cancelled via the Flexible Workflow Administration app, the order becomes stuck at "Order Approval in Process".

Recommended approch:

Step 1.  Open the Flexible Workflow Administration app (requires Administrator role). Search for WO 4031801. You are looking for the instance state, it will show as "In Process", "Error", "Completed", or "Cancelled". If it shows nothing at all, that points to Cause A (agent determination failure before the work item was even created).

Step 2. Using the "Work Items without Agents" app, you can manage work item instances that do not have an agent assigned. If there is a configuration error, no agent can be determined and the work item remains without an agent. Open this app and filter for maintenance order. If the item is here, the agent determination for Team Lead step has failed.

Step 3. Open the Workflows in Status Error app. If WO 4031801 appears here, use Resume after Error (not Restart). This re-runs the workflow from the failure point without resetting the order status.

Step 4. Emergency Recovery via API (if above fails)

SAP KBA 3614398 documents the API-based recovery for exactly this scenario. Rejecting via API moves the order back to a state where the planner can edit it, fix the data, and resubmit. Approving via API moves it forward if the content is already correct. Choose based on whether the WO needs rework.

 

Second issue:  reject not cleanly returning to workflow initiator

When an approver clicks Reject in My Inbox, the order changes to "Rejected (Order)" and the planner can then edit and resubmit the order for approval. The standard behavior is:

  • Order moves to Rejected (Order) subphase
  • Order is unlocked for editing by the planner
  • Planner must manually resubmit when rework is done

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-asset-management/planning-and-approving-maintenance_da03cb70-7173-43ed-9af1-a20fda3dea76

The standard does not do automatically notify the workflow initiator or route a task back to them.

The Flexible Workflow for maintenance orders uses Exception Handling in the workflow step configuration to define what happens on rejection. In the exception handling section of the workflow step, configure what happens when a recipient rejects.

Access app Manage Workflows for Maintenance Orders, in workflow step's Exception Handling section, for the "Rejected" exception:

  • Set the action to "Cancel Workflow" (this correctly sets the order to Rejected and ends the workflow cleanly)
  • Do not use "Restart the Workflow" for a reject-and-rework scenario, this sends the order back to the approver inbox, not to the planner

entry.title: Re: Research: Unmanaged Transactional Buffer For RESTful Application Programming Model
entry.content:
Hello, @manichand_ale

The main idea is that all operations, including creation, must be executed over a transactional buffer. This means that if we want to check for the existence of an entry in the database, we must first attempt to load the instance for the provided key into the buffer. Then, we check all creation keys to see whether they already exist in the buffer. If they do, it is a duplicate error; if they do not, we insert the new creation entry into the transactional buffer.

Check please how I have implemented operation in my example of buffer:
https://github.com/IlyaPrusakou/abap-rap-example.git

src/zpru_um_po/zpru_um_po_implmtns/zbp_pru_u_purcorderhdr_tp.clas.locals_imp.abap

entry.title: Re: 543转包库存的自动批次确定如何配置
entry.content:
Hello!

When posting a Goods Receipt for a Subcontract Purchase Order and selecting the Stock Determination icon for a component, the error "Stock Determination not possible" occurs because Search Procedure YB0001 is not assigned to Movement Type 543 O in SSCUI 102148 (Batch Determination for Inventory Management). Details in KBA 3137412.

Step 1.  Activate batch management in the Material Master:

Ensure the material has a batch class of class type 023 assigned with the characteristic for FIFO sorting, use standard characteristic LOBM_LWEDT (Date of Last Goods Receipt) for FIFO by receipt date

Step 2. Assign Search Procedure YB0001 to Movement Type 543 O. This is the critical step confirmed by SAP KBA 3137412. YB0001 is the SAP pre-delivered standard search procedure for Inventory Management batch determination in Public Cloud.

The entry must include:

  • Movement Type: 543
  • Special Stock: O (Subcontracting stock at vendor)
  • Search Procedure: YB0001
  • Activate the check indicator

Step 3.  Create Sort Sequence for FIFO (If Not Already Present)

  • Characteristic: LOBM_LWEDT (Date of Last Goods Receipt)
  • Sort direction: Ascending (oldest batch first = FIFO)
  • Assign this sort sequence to the relevant strategy type within YB0001.

Step 4.  Create Condition Records, this is the master data setup in Manage Search Strategies - Inventory Management app. Create a search strategy record for each batch-managed component:

  • Movement Type: 543
  • Special Stock: O
  • Plant: your plant
  • Material: the component material
  • Strategy Type: select the FIFO strategy from YB0001
  • Valid from/to dates

This record tells the system: "when movement type 543 O triggers for this material in this plant, apply the FIFO batch search strategy."

entry.title: Re: 供应商发票过账的最后更改日期
entry.content:
Hello!

There is no dedicated "last change date/time" field in the A_SupplierInvoice entity set of API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV that specifically captures the timestamp of when an invoice transitioned from Parked to Posted status.

Fields Available in A_SupplierInvoice Related to Date/Time

  • Document Date: Date on the vendor invoice document
  • Posting Date: Accounting posting date set on the invoice,this is business date, not system timestamp
  • Entry Date: Date the document was originally entered/created in SAP No — creation only
  • SupplierInvoiceStatus:  Current status: blank = Posted, A = Parked, B = Parked+Completed. No timestamp, status value only

 Use the accounting document API - once a parked invoice is posted, a corresponding FI accounting document is created. Use the Journal Entries API (API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV or API_OPLACCTGDOCITEMCUBE_SRV) to query the accounting document linked to the supplier invoice. The accounting document carries CreationDate and CreationTime which represent the timestamp when the document was posted.

For extracting "when did this invoice move from parked to posted," the most practical approach in Public Cloud is to filter invoices with SupplierInvoiceStatus = blank (= Posted) from A_SupplierInvoice.  For those invoices, use the Accounting Document API to retrieve the CreationDate of the corresponding FI document, this timestamp reflects when the posting action occurred.

entry.title: SMTP mail configuration in SSC system for DEFECT management tool - SOLMAN
entry.content:
PART – 1

Usually, we require SMTP server details (SMTP Host, Port, allowed sender, SMTP Auth credentials) to configure SMTP in ABAP instance.

So, for that we must request for the SMTP Auth account to the mail address <internet@abc.corp> Email/Network team.

SMTP AUTH request process details link: https://internet.abc.corp/services/smtp/workflow/smtp-auth-req/

And PDF documentation as Example : https://wiki.one.int.sap/wiki/display/itprm/SMTP+Server?preview=%2F2258032954%2F2258032953%2FSMTP-auth-account-request.pdf

 Below are the details we received from Email/Network team based on our requirement.

Host: mail.abc.corp
- Port: 465
  SSL: required
or
- Port: 587
  STARTTLS: required
Username: sdc-defect-mgmt-mails
Password: owners can use https://internet.abc.corp/change-pw to set it
(choose the account from the dropdown)
Allowed sender: noreply+SDC_Defect_Mgmt@abc.corp
Recipient scope: SAP-internal only
Additional information:
-----------------------
The CA for verifying the relay's server certificate is available at
(1st linked file in section 'SAP Global PKI Authority Information Access
/ CA Certificates').
For information on the relay IP addresses, network ACLs and how to test
the connectivity, see

Part - 2

Settings for the Profile Parameters (Transaction RZ10)

You must adjust the profile of the SAP Web Application Server if you want to use the SMTP function.

The SAPconnect send job can only be scheduled for servers on which SMTP has been activated. For this reason, you must activate SMTP for all the SAP system's application servers.

The following profile parameters must be configured. The placeholder <*> stands for the sequence number (starting at zero) of frequently occurring parameters.

icm/server_port_<*> = PROT=SMTP,PORT=<port>

Parameter icm/server_port_<*> opens a TCP/IP port for receiving mails through the SMTP plug-in.

<port> describes the number of the port that is to be used. The port cannot yet be used by another program on this host. If no mails are to be received in this SAP system (including notifications about the status), set < port > to 0.

Optionally, you can use TIMEOUT to define a maximum waiting time (in seconds) to be applied until the mail server responds.

is/SMTP/virt_host_<*> = <host>:<port>,<port>,...;

 This parameter defines a Virtual Host for receiving mails. If all incoming mails (including status notifications) are to be received and processed by one single client of the SAP system, this parameter is not required. In this instance, is/SMTP/virt_host_0 = *:*; is used by default. If multiple clients are to be used as recipients, a virtual host has to be created for each of these clients. <host> describes the name of the host to which the incoming mails are addressed. You can enter * here if the mails are to be sent independently of the host being addressed.

<port> describes the number of the port to which the incoming mails are addressed-requisites to be followed as part of configuration.

 For UNIX, the profile parameters may look like this:

rdisp/start_icman = true

icm/server_port_0 = PROT=HTTP,PORT=1080  #dont change if already configured with other port number.

icm/server_port_1 = PROT=HTTPS,PORT=1443  #dont change if already configured with other port number.

icm/server_port_2 = PROT=SMTP,PORT=< >,TIMEOUT=180 #Port number always send by the email/Network team.

is/SMTP/virt_host_0 = *:25000

is/SMTP/virt_host_1 = *:25001

User Maintenance (transaction SU01)

  • Service user

You must create a service user for processing incoming mails in every SAP system client in which you want to receive mails (or status notifications).

Call transaction SU01 to create a user with type Service and assign the profile S_A.SCON to this user.

  • User addresses

Every user that is to receive e-mails in an SAP system requires an Internet mail address.

This is also used as a sender address. Enter the Internet mail address for a user in transaction SU01, under E-Mail on the tab page Address.

Assigning the Client (Transaction SICF):

Every SAP system client that is to receive and process incoming mails (or status notifications) must have an SMTP server.

One SMTP server ( SAPconnect) has already been created in transaction SICF and is delivered with every SAP system. Use this to create the first client and then create another SMTP server for each additional client. Configure the following settings on the SMTP servers:

  • Host Data

Enter the sequence number of a parameter is/SMTP/virt_host_<*> from the profile.

If you have only created one client and therefore have not specified this parameter type, enter 0.

  • Service data

Enter the client to which the mails received through the virtual mail host are to be forwarded,

and then enter the logon data of the system user that you created for the incoming mails.

  • Handler list

Enter CL_SMTP_EXT_SAPCONNECT at position 1.

Note

As of SAP Web AS 6.20, each SMTP server has to be activated ( Start of the navigation path Service/Virt.

Host Next navigation step Activate End of the navigation path) after being created or changed.Inactive servers are grayed out.

SAPconnect Administration (Transaction SCOT)

You must configure SAPconnect settings for every client that is used for sending processes.

Default domain:

Under --> Settings à Default Domain.

define the domain of the SAP system client. This allows for the following to take place:

  • The SMTP plug-in logs on to the mail server using the domain as ID.
  • The message ID of the outbound e-mails is assembled with this domain.
  • If an SAP user who does not have an Internet mail address sends an e-mail,

  a sender address consisting of the abc username and this domain is generated.

Node:

You can create one or multiple SMTP nodes to contact different mail/fax/SMS servers, for example.

The following steps need to be carried out to configure an SMTP node so that sending Internet mail is possible:

  1. Double-click the node name.
  2. Choose Node in Use
  3. In the Mail Host and Mail Port fields, specify the mail server to which the outbound mails are to be sent.
  4. Next to Internet, choose Settings.
  5. Enter the address area of the recipient addresses that are to be reached using this node. In general, * is used if all E-Mails are to be sent using SMTP.
  6. Enter the output format for SAP documents. We recommend that you use the following settings:

Confirm the selection.

entry.title: Re: Not able to get CDS for tax rates based on tax jurisd...
entry.content:
Hello!

Rather than attempting to look up jurisdiction tax rates from a master data CDS view (which does not exist), we recommend switching from Workaround 1 to Workaround 2 as described in Note 3636847.

Workaround 2 uses BAdI MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT / MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_ITEM_EXT, which fires during output determination after the PO is already saved. At this point, the tax engine has already calculated the full jurisdiction-level tax amounts. These calculated amounts are available via I_PurOrdItmPricingElementAPI01, which is confirmed to be populated at this stage (KBA 3008187).

This means:

  • no independent rate lookup is needed
  • no CDS view for jurisdiction rates is required
  • read the already-calculated condition type values and pass them to the output form

As stated in the SAP note, MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT / MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_ITEM_EXT does not update custom fields at the database level. Changes are reflected only on the output form (PDF/email).

If the individual jurisdiction levels (state, county, city) are required separately on the output form rather than as a single total, each level's condition type must be mapped to a separate custom field.

entry.title: Re: Objective Delete Notification for Private Goals
entry.content:
Hi @mansinagpure,

There is no supported way in SAP SuccessFactors PMGM to send the Objective Delete Notification for both public and private goals without granting employees the "private-access" permission in the Goal Plan XML.

Here is why this is a limitation:

How the notification works for private goals: The Goal Delete Notification is triggered when a manager deletes a goal from an employee's goal plan. For private goals specifically, the system condition is explicit: "If the applicable notification email is enabled and the user has permission to access the private goals, notifications will be sent."

You may consider raising this as an enhancement request via the SAP Customer Influence portal.

entry.title: Re: External billing document request_Project billing req...
entry.content:
Hello!

The EBDR channel (Excel upload in Manage Billing Document Requests app, or BillingDocumentRequestRequest_In via SAP_COM_0176) and the standard PS billing flow (PBR - BDR) are two separate channels. When you reference a PBR in the EBDR template, the PBR items are not used. The PBR item consumption only happens when BDRs are created via the standard 4E9 project billing flow.

The BDR API supports reading partner data but modifying the bill-to party on a project-generated BDR is restricted. The bill-to party is derived from the sales order / billing item and can’t be overridden via the BDR API after creation.

Alternatives to be used:

1. PBR OData API + post-creation BDR modification. Use the PBR OData API to trigger PBR/BDR creation, this correctly consumes PBR items and clears WIP/revenue via the standard flow. Then attempt to modify the bill-to party on the BDR via the BDR API before billing.

2. Modify bill-to party upstream on the Sales Order Item. Use API_SALES_ORDER_SRV to update partner function RE (bill-to party) on the sales order item before PBR creation. The modified partner then flows through PBR - BDR - Invoice via the standard process.

3. For passing custom field values, SAP has released process extensibility that allows custom fields to flow end-to-end from timesheet through PBR to billing document output.

Each alternative support:

EBDR Excel / SAP_COM_0176: does not consume PBR items, can modify bill-to party, limited custom field support.

PBR OData API (2302+): correctly consumes PBR items, bill-to party modification needs testing, custom fields via process extensibility.

Sales Order API (upstream modification): correctly consumes PBR items via standard flow, can modify bill-to party, custom fields via process extensibility.

entry.title: Creation of Joule skill using API
entry.content:
1. Add destination(mostly via basis team)

2.Add Action 

Choose an API Source ( Depending on API )

 

In my case my API was added into my System using /n/iwfnd/maint_serivce , So I have selected Odata  Destination , if it's RAP API use the second one.

Now select the destination(Destination is just the system you have integrated)

Enter the resource path ( from /sap/opu****  in case of maint_service or RAP )

then next and select/add all the entity which you wanted to use as action and PUBLISH/RELEASE 

You can test the action there as well.

Now let's go to Create joule skill.

Go to lobby and Click on create button select joule agent and skill , Provide any contextual name 

This type of Interface will be visible to you , Click on create again select joule skill , Give any name along with description 

This type of interface will be displayed to you , now click on ADD(+) Button

Then select Call Action and again click on Browse all Action 

search your action add it.  If your action is dependent on parameters such(Let's say your api provides response based on certain values ypu passed

Tap here , In Parameters you can add your input skills.

Now Tap on your action and go to input parameters 

Map it accordingly all the mandatory fields 

Only this will be enough to call API

But as we wanted to display our result in chatbot itself 

Go To END and add output skills , Map it accordingly again but with result ( Because we need to display result )

Now save and Test (Destination System),

You will be able to see result( Let me share you some sample result )

You can see , Chat results only values select and mapped in output skills where as when you tapped on api call at right you will be able to see complete result.

 

FAQ- Prabhatm580@gmail.com

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
entry.content: ad8759ddc990d29d89ad227a9555d3a191d8c39b8e5dbfe5e740d12151bbbedd

entry.title: Re: 我在银行清账时显示没有容差组，问题该怎么解决
entry.content:
Hi avally12345,

Short answer: Even you did not stated the exact message no., the "no tolerance group" error during clearing most probably means no tolerance group is maintained/assigned for the company code — fix it in SSCUI 102832 (Define Tolerance Groups for Employees); only raise a support case if the error persists after that.

How do I resolve the "no tolerance group" error during bank clearing?

Maintain a tolerance group for the affected company code in SSCUI 102832.

The clearing posting checks an employee (user) tolerance group for the company code; if none exists, the posting is blocked. Open Define Tolerance Groups for Employees (SSCUI 102832), ensure at least one tolerance group exists and is assigned to the company code, and that fields such as Amount per document are filled. Reference: KBA 3165431 – "No amount tolerance range entered for company code XXXX".

Verify: Confirm the exact message no. on your screen (e.g. F5103). If the message instead mentions a customer/supplier tolerance, the gap is in Define Tolerances for Customers and Suppliers instead — see SAP Help – Tolerance Limits.

Which configuration and settings should I confirm to ensure tolerance groups are correctly maintained and assigned?

Confirm three things, in this order:

  1. Employee tolerance group (primary): A group entry must exist for the company code in SSCUI 102832 with the amount/percentage limits populated.
  2. User-to-group assignment: In Public Cloud the on-prem assignment transaction OB57 is not exposed as an SSCUI, and the blank group is assigned to all users by default. So in practice, the entry you create in SSCUI 102832 for the company code must carry a blank group key to cover all users.
  3. Tolerances used in automatic clearing: If the error appears during automatic clearing (job / Clear Open Items Automatically), the relevant tolerance SSCUIs are 102832 together with 102323 / 102923. Reference: KBA 3364246.

Important:

  • Bank sub-accounts / clearing accounts are typically cleared manually (Clear G/L Account) or via Reprocess Bank Statement Items; the tolerance check fires on the clearing posting itself. Reference: SAP Help – Clearing Process.
  • A non-blank user group assignment (anything beyond '  ') requires expert configuration - it is not self-serviceable.

Do I need a support case for detailed troubleshooting?

No — not as a first step. Maintain SSCUI 102832 first; this resolves the standard occurrence per KBA 3165431.

Raise a case only if: the error persists after a correct, assigned tolerance group exists, or you need a specific (non-blank) user→group assignment. Use component FI-FIO-GL-TRA-2CL (G/L posting/clearing) or FI-BL-PT-BA-2CL (bank statement), and attach the full message number and the SSCUI 102832 screenshot for the company code.

Summary: Create/assign a tolerance group with a blank group key for the affected company code in SSCUI 102832 and populate its limits; this is the standard fix per KBA 3165431. Confirm the message isn't pointing at the customer/supplier tolerance instead. Open a support case only if the error survives correct configuration or you need a non-default user assignment (expert config via CEE/CSM).

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Need to complete Sales order inVA02
entry.content: If the old SOs have completed overall status, then it is possible to remove incompletion logs via report sdvbuv00. See note 106567.
